title: Calling an Rcpp function with mirai
text: Within project, compute kernel written C++ Rcpp R function distributes chunks work across mirai daemons. packaging kernel, daemon reaches compiled function directly namespace-qualified call, copying source recompilation workers. example task bootstrap mean: kernel draws bootstrap resamples returns mean , R driver splits total number resamples across available daemons.

title: Calling an Rcpp function with mirai
text: kernel lives src/bootstrap_mean.cpp. draws reps resamples x replacement returns mean , producing bootstrap distribution. uses R’s random number generator R::unif_rand(), matters parallel case described .
code: #include <Rcpp.h>  // [[Rcpp::export]] Rcpp::NumericVector bootstrap_mean(Rcpp::NumericVector x, int reps) {   int n = x.size();   Rcpp::NumericVector out(reps);    for (int b = 0; b < reps; b++) {     double sum = 0.0;     for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {       int idx = (int)(R::unif_rand() * n);   // draw an index in [0, n)       sum += x[idx];     }     out[b] = sum / n;   }    return out; }

title: Calling an Rcpp function with mirai
text: parallel_bootstrap_mean() driver. keeping design mirai, set daemons : reads number connected daemons mirai::status() , none, runs kernel current session. daemon pool present, splits requested number resamples one chunk per daemon distributes mirai::mirai_map(). work sent package worker bootstrap_chunk() (inline function), pieces combined back single bootstrap distribution [.flat] collector. run parallel, user starts pool daemons first, resets finished:
code: parallel_bootstrap_mean = function(x, reps = 10000L, chunks = NULL) {    reps = as.integer(reps)    # mirai leaves daemon set up to the caller. With no connected daemons,   # evaluate the compiled kernel once in the current session.   n_daemons = mirai::status()$connections   if (n_daemons < 1L) {     return(bootstrap_mean(x, reps))   }    # One task per daemon unless the caller asks for a different split.   if (is.null(chunks)) {     chunks = n_daemons   }   chunks = max(1L, min(as.integer(chunks), reps))    # Split `reps` as evenly as possible into `chunks` pieces.   sizes = diff(round(seq(0L, reps, length.out = chunks + 1L)))   sizes = sizes[sizes > 0L]    # Distribute the pieces across the daemons. `bootstrap_chunk` is a package   # function, so only a reference to it travels to each daemon, which then   # loads the package to reach the compiled kernel.   mirai::mirai_map(     sizes,     bootstrap_chunk,     .args = list(x = x)   )[.flat] }  # Internal worker run on each daemon. Kept at the top level (its environment is # the package namespace) so that mirai serialises a reference to it instead of a # copy of the caller's frame. bootstrap_chunk = function(reps, x) {   RcppMirai::bootstrap_mean(x, reps) } library("RcppMirai")  mirai::daemons(4)                                  # start 4 local daemons boot = parallel_bootstrap_mean(rnorm(1000), reps = 1e5) mirai::daemons(0)                                  # reset when finished  quantile(boot, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))

title: Calling an Rcpp function with mirai
text: daemon starts user packages attached, work sent must reach compiled code . namespace-qualified call RcppMirai::bootstrap_mean() needed use compiled function installed package daemon, since :: loads package compiled code first use. nothing special Rcpp code beyond installing package, point confirmed mirai discussion package development mirai package vignette. function handed mirai_map() package worker bootstrap_chunk(), defined top level package rather inline. environment package namespace, mirai transmits reference . function written inline inside parallel_bootstrap_mean() instead capture call’s environment copy , including x, every daemon, wasteful contrary explicit-dependency design mirai. Daemon set left caller. mirai rely global options environment variables, letting user call mirai::daemons() keeps function composable mirai work session. daemon also draws independent random number stream, keeps resamples drawn parallel statistically sound; passing seed mirai::daemons() makes streams reproducible fixed number chunks. Two notes apply compiled code particular. kernel threaded, example OpenMP, every daemon launch threads, total thread count becomes number daemons multiplied threads per daemon; keep kernel single-threaded, cap threads, avoid oversubscribing machine. exported examples also run current session default start daemons inside (interactive()) guard, package checks spawn extra processes.

title: Bootstrap the Mean in Parallel with mirai — parallel_bootstrap_mean
text: Daemon set intentionally left caller, keeping design mirai. Start pool mirai::daemons() calling function run parallel, reset mirai::daemons(0) finished. number connected daemons read mirai::status(). work sent daemons package worker bootstrap_chunk() rather inline function. worker lives package namespace, mirai transmits reference , copy function's environment, daemon loads package (compiled code) run . Defining worker inline instead capture whole calling frame send every daemon. daemons running, one draws independent random number stream. Pass seed mirai::daemons() make streams reproducible fixed number chunks. -session fallback draws ordinary session stream, values match parallel path.

title: Bootstrap the Mean in Parallel with mirai — parallel_bootstrap_mean
code: x = rnorm(500)  # With no daemon pool the kernel runs in the current session. boot = parallel_bootstrap_mean(x, reps = 1000) str(boot) #>  num [1:1000] -0.06243 -0.04264 -0.07245 0.02076 0.00907 ...  # Set up daemons to draw the resamples in parallel. if (interactive()) {   mirai::daemons(2)   boot = parallel_bootstrap_mean(x, reps = 1e5)   mirai::daemons(0)   quantile(boot, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)) }
